core_mechanism: Birkhoff-type multiplicative factorization of an algebra homomorphism into two components (a 'finite/regular' part and a 'divergent/pole' part) induced by a linear projection / Rota-Baxter operator that splits the target algebra, computed recursively through the coproduct of a graded connected Hopf algebra
brief_reason: The abstract frames its method as a differential-algebra variation of the Algebraic Birkhoff Decomposition (the Connes-Kreimer Hopf-algebra factorization used to separate divergent from finite parts) and applies it to extract renormalized values.
